texto: No fiction is good fiction unless it is true to life, and yet no life is worth relating unless it be a life out of the ordinary; and then it seems improbable like fiction.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a William Gerhardie: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “No fiction is good fiction unless it is true to life, and yet no life is worth relating unless it…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, fiction, unless pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

texto: If a book is worth reading at all it is worth reading more than once. Suspense is the lowest of excitants designed to take your breath away when the brain and heart crave to linger in nobler enjoyment. Suspense drags you on appreciation causes you to linger.
explicacao: Citação de William Gerhardie sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “If a book is worth reading at all it is worth reading more than once. Suspense is the lowest of e…” articula amor, books, reading em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como dia-chuvoso e reading.
